# conversations

## Plugin reference

- Description: Conversation domain: shared hooks and client-side API. Conversation domain: shared server code and types; view plugins live under `plugins/`.
- Load-bearing: yes

- Sub-plugins:
  - **`agents`** тАФ Named agent definitions that launch conversations. Named agent definitions that launch conversations.
  - **`all-conversations`** тАФ All-conversations app pane: a server-delegated DataView (filter/sort/search/keyset over every conversation) reachable from the agent-manager sidebar. Global conversations query handler (filter/sort/search/keyset over conversations_v) + the scalar revision-tick live resource that keeps the All-conversations DataView window fresh.
  - **`conversation-category`** тАФ Per-conversation category chip in the sidebar row and conversation toolbar. Auto-classified by Haiku after each turn; manual override via the toolbar chip's popover. Classifies each conversation into one of a configurable list of categories using Haiku. Surfaces the result as a chip in the sidebar row and the conversation toolbar.
  - **`conversation-preprompt`** тАФ Header chip showing the preprompt the conversation's task was launched with; a popover reveals the full instruction text. Sidebar rows show the preprompt's icon (resolved live from the library, with a default-glyph fallback). Snapshots the launching task's selected preprompt (id + title + text) onto each newly created conversation, surfaced as a chip in the conversation header.
  - **`conversation-progress`** тАФ 4-step progress bar (research тЖТ plan тЖТ implementation тЖТ pushed) in the conversation toolbar and sidebar chip. Tracks each conversation through four phases (research тЖТ design тЖТ implementation тЖТ pushed) via git heuristics: no files = research, only research/** = design, any other file = implementation, push event = pushed.
  - **`conversation-ui`** тАФ Umbrella for visual primitives that render a Conversation. Sub-plugins ship the actual components (item rows/chips, future cards/mentions/etc.).
    - Plugins:
      - **`item`** тАФ Visual primitive for rendering a Conversation as a row or inline chip. Used by every surface that lists conversations.
  - **`conversation-view`** тАФ Conversation pane host. Header and prompt bar are slot-driven; Conversation.Header hosts title and toolbar chips.
    - Plugins:
      - **`action-bar`** тАФ Hosts the Conversation.ActionBar slot тАФ action buttons rendered in the JSONL viewer header.
      - **`allow-monitor`** тАФ Flags when an agent has created an allow-file (.allow-main, .allow-postgres) to bypass security guards.
      - **`branch`** тАФ Forks the current Claude session into a background conversation with the typed draft as the opening prompt.
      - **`code`** тАФ Meta plugin hosting code-related contributions for a conversation (edited files, viewer, etc.). Tracks edited files in the conversation's worktree via the live-state primitive.
        - Plugins:
          - **`docs-button`** тАФ Toolbar button that opens a sidebar listing edited markdown design docs in the conversation worktree.
          - **`file-pane`** тАФ Hosts the file-peek pane and the FilePane.Renderer slot.
            - Plugins:
              - **`diff`** тАФ Side-by-side diff of the file vs HEAD in the conversation's worktree.
              - **`image`** тАФ Image preview for .png, .jpg, .gif, .webp, .svg, and similar files.
              - **`markdown`** тАФ Rendered markdown preview for .md and .mdx files.
              - **`raw`** тАФ Plain file renderer with syntax highlighting. Fallback tab for any text file.
      - **`commits-graph`** тАФ Toolbar chip showing commits ahead/behind main; opens a side pane with the chain of commits between merge-base and HEAD. Toolbar chip showing commits ahead/behind main; opens a side pane with the chain of commits between merge-base and HEAD.
      - **`dependencies`** тАФ Unified prompt-bar button showing blocked-by and blocking dependency counts with per-direction edit popovers.
      - **`dependent-count`** тАФ Shows the count of tasks transitively blocked by the current conversation's task.
      - **`drop-and-exit`** тАФ Exit-menu entry that marks the top task as dropped and closes the conversation.
      - **`drop-dependents`** тАФ Exit-menu entry that drops the task and all its transitive dependents, then closes the conversation.
      - **`exit`** тАФ Exit-menu entry that closes the conversation without changing any task state.
      - **`exit-menu`** тАФ Ghost icon button next to Push & Exit that opens a menu of exit actions (hold, exit, drop, drop dependents). Hosts the ExitMenu.Item slot each action contributes to.
      - **`fork-conversation`** тАФ Toolbar buttons (+Sonnet / +Opus) that spin up a new conversation in the same worktree.
      - **`fork-session`** тАФ Toolbar buttons (+Sonnet / +Opus) that fork the current conversation via `claude --resume <id> --fork-session`.
      - **`header`** тАФ Hosts the Conversation.Header slot тАФ all header segments (title, chips) rendered in the PaneChrome title area.
      - **`hold-and-exit`** тАФ Exit-menu entry that marks the task as held and closes the conversation.
      - **`jsonl-viewer`** тАФ Renders the raw Claude JSONL session log as the conversation's main content. Hosts the JsonlViewer.EventRenderer slot for child plugins to render specific event kinds. Parses Claude's raw JSONL session log and streams it as structured events via the jsonl-events resource.
        - Plugins:
          - **`assistant-text`** тАФ Renders assistant text events in the JSONL viewer, with optional markdown rendering.
          - **`assistant-thinking`** тАФ Renders assistant thinking blocks in the JSONL viewer as collapsible sections.
          - **`attachment`** [12 sub-plugins] тАФ Renders attachment JSONL events with subtype dispatch to per-attachment renderer plugins.
          - **`code-listing`** тАФ Renders `cat -n`-formatted file content with syntax highlighting and a line-number gutter. Shared by the Read tool renderer and the edited-file attachment renderer.
          - **`collapsible-card`** тАФ Disclosure-card primitive: chevron trigger, optional interactive sibling aside (never nested), and a collapsible body. One uniform chrome; semantic accents live in the label, the error flag, and the call-site className. Pure chrome тАФ it depends on no domain component.
          - **`event-counter`** тАФ Displays the total event count in the conversation toolbar.
          - **`fields-card`** тАФ Shared appearance for a headline + truncating summary preview + fold-out key/value field list. Used by the queued task-notification card and the native task-notification row so the two never diverge.
          - **`file-path`** тАФ Clickable file path component with RTL ellipsis, copy button, and file-peek pane integration.
          - **`investigate-event`** тАФ Contributes the add-a-renderer row action to every JSONL transcript row whose nearest dispatch fell back (unhandled event kind, tool name, or attachment subtype), launching an agent briefed to implement the missing renderer for that dispatch key.
          - **`message-toc`** тАФ Floating table of contents listing user messages for quick navigation.
          - **`meta-prompt`** тАФ Renders harness-injected prompt turns (loop/queue wakeups, resumes) distinctly from human user messages.
          - **`preprompt`** тАФ Renders the launch special-instructions (preprompt) block as a collapsible section in the JSONL viewer.
          - **`queue-operation`** тАФ Renders Claude Code prompt-queue events (enqueue/dequeue/remove) in the JSONL viewer.
          - **`queued-prompt-card`** тАФ Shared appearance for a queued prompt (a message the user parked while the agent was busy). Used by both the queued_command attachment and the prompt-queue enqueue row so the two never diverge.
          - **`row-actions`** тАФ Owns the JSONL transcript's hover-revealed row-action strip: the JsonlRowActions.Item slot, the per-event context, and the shared action-button styling. Sits below collapsible-card so card chrome can host the strip without a cycle.
          - **`summary`** тАФ Renders summary separator events in the JSONL viewer.
          - **`system`** тАФ Renders system events in the JSONL viewer.
          - **`task-notification`** тАФ Renders background task completion notifications in the JSONL viewer.
          - **`teammate-message`** тАФ Renders messages relayed from other Claude sessions (<teammate-message> blocks) distinctly from human user messages.
          - **`tool-call`** [11 sub-plugins] тАФ Renders paired tool-call events with exact/pattern/fallback dispatch to per-tool renderer plugins.
          - **`user-image`** тАФ Renders inline image thumbnails for user-image events.
          - **`user-text`** тАФ Renders user text events in the JSONL viewer.
      - **`launch-prompts`** тАФ Pre-configured prompts that launch a new background conversation in the same worktree. Pre-configured prompts that launch a new background conversation in the same worktree.
      - **`markdown-extensions`** тАФ Conversation-scoped markdown enhancers: file-links, inline code enhancements, and image proxying.
      - **`model`** тАФ Displays the conversation model as a colored chip in the toolbar.
      - **`new-child-task`** тАФ Deprecated тАФ functionality merged into the Improve button via ambient relate context.
      - **`notes`** тАФ Free-form per-conversation notes, auto-saved to the server. Always visible when notes exist; toggle via the note button. Per-conversation free-form notes, auto-saved to the server.
      - **`op-status`** тАФ Banner above the prompt input showing the worktree's in-flight build/push, with elapsed time and a 'queued / waiting for lock' phase for pushes. Also a sidebar row chip flagging the same op (Building / Pushing / Waiting for lock). Watches the per-worktree build/push op markers and pushes them to a live-state resource. Renders a banner above the prompt input showing the in-flight operation (build / push / push queued waiting for lock) with elapsed time.
      - **`open-app`** тАФ Opens the conversation's namespace at `http://<id>.localhost:9000/`.
      - **`pending-turn`** тАФ Owner of the entire turn-send lifecycle: a durable (localStorage) per-conversation pending-turn state machine (sending тЖТ posted тЖТ queued/sent, failed-post, unconfirmed) that POSTs the turn, verifies delivery against the transcript (normalized-text match), files a report when a 200'd turn never lands, and renders the per-record PendingTurnCard. The prompt-input calls sendPendingTurn on Enter; the jsonl-viewer drives reconcilePendingTurns on every events change. No slot contributions.
      - **`prompt-input`** тАФ Free-form text input at the bottom of the conversation view. Enter sends a turn; fork buttons reuse the draft as the new conversation's initial prompt.
      - **`prompt-templates`** тАФ Template chips inside the prompt editor that prepend text to the draft. A floating icon expands on hover to reveal available templates. Named template chips that prepend text to the conversation prompt editor for editing before sending.
      - **`push-and-exit`** тАФ Toolbar button that asks Claude to push the branch and close the conversation; surfaces Claude's flag if it has anything to raise.
      - **`push-counter`**
      - **`push-profiling`** тАФ Toolbar button showing the build/push/check op Gantt scoped to the conversation's worktree.
      - **`resume`** тАФ Toolbar button that resumes a gone conversation via `claude --resume <claude-id>`.
      - **`status`** тАФ Displays the conversation status as a colored badge in the toolbar.
      - **`tasks-panel`** тАФ Toolbar button that toggles the task pane (tree + detail) for the conversation's task.
      - **`terminal-pane`** тАФ Toolbar button that opens a right pane attaching to the conversation's tmux session.
      - **`turn-summary`** тАФ Inline card above the prompt input showing a Haiku-generated summary of the latest assistant turn, with caveats and suggested actions. After every assistant turn, runs Haiku on the (user, assistant) pair to produce a one-line summary, caveats list, and actions list. Renders above the prompt input.
      - **`vscode`** тАФ Opens the conversation's worktree in VSCode.
  - **`conversations-view`** тАФ Sidebar list of all conversations.
    - Plugins:
      - **`data-view`** тАФ Umbrella for the DataView conversation-list sidebar: owns the merged multi-source DataView surface (one config, one unified switcher) mounted directly by the conversations-view mount point. Per-source sub-plugins (Queue, History) contribute into SidebarSources.
        - Plugins:
          - **`history`** тАФ Contributes the History list (a server-delegated bundle reusing the all-conversations query infra) as the History source of the merged conversation-sidebar DataView.
          - **`queue`** тАФ Contributes the priority Queue (status group-by sections, task-group aggregation, and neighbor-based manual-order drag over the queue's live data/mutation layer) as the Queue source of the merged conversation-sidebar DataView.
      - **`grouped`** тАФ Conversation-group persistence (tables + addMemberToGroup) backing the improve plugin's group-on-launch. No UI.
      - **`queue`** тАФ Queue classification + reorder logic (classifyQueue / applyReorder) consumed by the DataView Queue tab. Ranks seeded once on creation (newest first); pinned top conversation is the user's current focus. Stable-rank global queue. Ranks seeded once on creation (newest first). Pinned top conversation persists as the user's current focus.
  - **`effort-provider`** тАФ Registry mapping thinking-mode (effort) levels to Claude CLI delivery (--effort flag / --settings ultracode) and display metadata. Reusable EffortSelect picker.
  - **`hibernation`** тАФ Records conversation selection so idle hibernation can reset the idle timer and transparently resume. Idle-conversation hibernation policy: a scheduled idle-kill job, the viewed/resume endpoint, and the global hibernation config.
  - **`model-provider`** тАФ Registry mapping logical ConversationModel IDs to pinned Claude CLI flags and display metadata. Registry mapping logical ConversationModel IDs to pinned Claude CLI flags and display metadata.
  - **`pane-restore`** тАФ Saves and restores the pane route per conversation using localStorage.
  - **`preprompts`** тАФ Settings library of system-prompt snippets and a reusable picker for selecting a task's preprompt. Library of named instruction snippets prepended to a task's agent first user turn as a <special_instructions> block.
  - **`recover`** тАФ Sidebar entry + pane listing recently-closed conversations with restore buttons. Batch-restore recently-closed conversations that were killed by a crash.
  - **`runtime-api`** тАФ Stub placeholder for running Claude via the Anthropic Agent SDK (not yet implemented).
  - **`runtime-tmux`** тАФ Runs Claude CLI sessions inside tmux panes.
  - **`session-chain`** тАФ Append-only mapping of a conversation to the ordered Claude session ids it has run under. Knows nothing about transcript files.
  - **`summary`** тАФ Toolbar button that opens a side pane with the Summarise action and the latest structured Sonnet summary (phase, flags, next action). On-demand structured summaries of conversations: phase, flags, next action. Curated by Sonnet via MCP. Append-only history.
  - **`transcript-api`** тАФ Agent API: GET /api/conversations/:id/transcript returns the ordered on-disk JSONL paths of a conversation's Claude session chain.
  - **`transcript-retention`** тАФ Keeps retained conversations' Claude session JSONL alive by refreshing their mtime daily тАФ active rows plus every conversation of a held task тАФ so Claude Code's cleanupPeriodDays sweep never deletes a transcript the user can still come back to.
  - **`transcript-watcher`** тАФ Single @parcel/watcher-based JSONL transcript watcher. Replaces two independent 500ms pollers with one fan-out subscription.

<!-- AUTOGENERATED:END -->
